texto: I like nature and I enjoy walking through the forest admiring its beauty and breathing not just the fresh air but, also the quietness and peacefulness of the place. There I feel serene. I feel I am accepted just the way I am whenever I arrive and for as long as I stay. Yes, there it doesn’t matter how I look, what country I come from, if I am from rich or poor family, what is my education, income, religion, sexual orientation and color of skin. It doesn’t even care if my hair looks messy and whether I wear the latest fashion cloths.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Nico J. Genes: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “I like nature and I enjoy walking through the forest admiring its beauty and breathing not just t…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que educacao, acceptance, acceptance-of-oneself-and-others pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de acceptance-of-oneself-and-others e diverse.
